dialogue.utterance: Unit 1 to 63-year-old female with chest pain on Richland Court. Patient refusing transport previously?
Hello, I'm Mark with the rescue team. Can I ask, what brought you to call 911 today?
Yes, I've been having lower stomach pain that's really severe, and I've had nausea and diarrhea a lot.
Okay, can you tell me your name and birthday?
I'm Rachel, born October 27th, 1958.
Great job. What part of [STATE] do we live in right now?
We're in [CITY], I think.
And what happened today that led you to call for help?
I've been having stomach pain all day.
We're about to check your breathing. Can you help with a few quick steps?
Her respirations are 18, within normal limits. Let me check the oxygen.
That's okay, let me see. Her SpO2 is down at 89, so we'll need to track that more closely.
Here, put this on. It will read your oxygen level by itself.
Pulse is 97, regular, and blood pressure is 177/90. Seems strong.
Can you feel the room, your hand, and the side of the cot?
Yes, I feel everything.
Understood-can you tell me a bit more about the pain? When did it start?
It began this morning and has gotten a lot worse. It's been going on for about 10 hours.
Does the pain move anywhere? For example, into the side or the back?
No, it stays right in my lower stomach. It's bad the whole time.
Can you describe the pain as sharp, dull, burning, or cramping?
I would say it's a generalized ache. It's just awful.
On a scale of 1 to 10, 10 being the worst, how bad is the pain?
It's a 10.
We need to know a bit about your health history. Have you had any past issues with your heart or lungs?
Yes, I had a heart attack. I also have high blood pressure and mild asthma.
Are you allergic to any medication or other substances?
Can't take morphine or penicillin. I got reactions before.
Okay, how long has this pain been constant?
Ten hours now. It was mild last night, but it's been getting stronger today.
Now checking pulse-97, regular rate.
And respirations are 18 per minute.
SpO2 at 89%.
Attach leads-EKG shows sinus rhythm.
I'll look over the abdomen now, but given your size, it might be difficult to assess fully.
We'll conduct a neurological check as well. Can you tell me what city we are in now?
We're in [CITY].
Blood sugar is undetectable.
We'll monitor it, but it may not be giving us results right now.
Let me obtain a 12-lead. Will clip on one more lead here.
Got the 12-lead now. It came back sinus rhythm-nothing immediately alarming.
From my findings, I'd like to proceed using the medical abdominal pain protocol, protocol 3-2.
Let me take vitals again now. Pulse is 102.
Respirations remain steady at 18.
Blood pressure now at 144/91, slightly lowered.
SpO2 at 96% now. Collecting one final EKG for consistency.
EKG was clear earlier, comes back normal now.
Second observation matches our first findings. I still believe medical protocol 3-2 applies.
How severe is the pain now on the same 1-10 scale?
It's still a 10, doesn't feel better or worse.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Any things we haven't discussed yet about the pain or your condition?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: No, I made everything clear.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: And you didn't vomit or lose consciousness?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: No, I just feel like I'm about to vomit again.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: We'll monitor for vomiting symptoms and pain location as you're moved.
Do you have a preferred hospital for treatment?
[COMPANY_NAME] if they can help.
Alright, that's our plan. We'll be heading to [COMPANY_NAME] for evaluation and care.
Load secured, no pain reported during positioning, and no new findings observed-transport initiated.
Any name or insurance card? For the record and hospital.
No insurance. My name is Rachel, but you probably needed the other stuff already.
Thank you, we have notes on that. Be ready when we arrive.
To clarify, are you alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation? This helps with documentation.
I'm Rachel, I'm in [CITY], it's April and I know I've been in pain all morning.
Breath sounds are normal bilaterally, both on the left and right.
Let's start the 4 leads for the EKG. I'll make sure the connections are snug as we begin.
Your alertness today seems to be good-A and O x4.
In addition to your heart history, you also have Coronary Artery Disease-correct?
Yes, that's part of my ongoing cardiac issues.
We just confirmed another 4-lead ECG monitoring session and hanging a 12-lead tracing.
